examples.title: Air Quality Index Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate AQI and determine pollution level category
examples.code:
// AQI Calculation Algorithm
BEGIN
 INPUT: Concentrations of pollutants [PM2.5, PM10, SO2, NO2, O3, CO]
 
 FOR each pollutant p IN pollutants:
 Calculate sub-index I_p using breakpoints
 I_p = ((I_high - I_low) / (C_high - C_low)) * (C_p - C_low) + I_low
 END FOR
 
 AQI = MAX(I_p) for all pollutants
 
 IF AQI <= 50 THEN
 Category = "Good" - Air quality is satisfactory
 ELSE IF AQI <= 100 THEN
 Category = "Moderate" - Acceptable
 ELSE IF AQI <= 200 THEN
 Category = "Poor" - May cause breathing discomfort
 ELSE IF AQI <= 300 THEN
 Category = "Very Poor" - Respiratory illness on prolonged exposure
 ELSE IF AQI <= 400 THEN
 Category = "Severe" - Serious health effects
 ELSE
 Category = "Hazardous" - Emergency conditions
 END IF
 
 OUTPUT: AQI value and Category
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates Air Quality Index by converting raw pollutant concentrations into sub-indices and taking the maximum value. The AQI category helps public understand health risks and take appropriate precautions.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of pollutants

examples.title: Waste Segregation System
examples.description: Algorithm for sorting waste into different categories for proper disposal
examples.code:
// Waste Segregation Algorithm
BEGIN
 INPUT: Waste item w
 
 IF w.material_type == "organic" THEN
 OUTPUT: "Biodegradable" bin
 // For composting
 ELSE IF w.material_type == "plastic" AND w.recyclable == TRUE THEN
 OUTPUT: "Recyclable" bin
 ELSE IF w.material_type == "plastic" AND w.recyclable == FALSE THEN
 OUTPUT: "Non-recyclable" bin
 ELSE IF w.material_type == "metal" OR w.material_type == "glass" THEN
 OUTPUT: "Recyclable" bin
 ELSE IF w.hazardous == TRUE THEN
 OUTPUT: "Hazardous waste" bin
 // For special treatment
 ELSE IF w.material_type == "paper" AND w.clean == TRUE THEN
 OUTPUT: "Recyclable paper" bin
 ELSE
 OUTPUT: "General waste" bin
 END IF
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm helps in proper waste segregation at source, which is crucial for effective recycling and safe disposal. Proper segregation increases recycling efficiency and reduces environmental harm.
